qualification.details: Part-Time Correspondent (PTC): PG Diploma/Degree in Journalism/Mass Media or a graduate with a minimum of two years of journalistic experience. Knowledge of computers and word processing. Possession of news-gathering devices.

salary_details.pay_scale: Retainership remuneration as per Prasar Bharati guidelines
salary_details.details: Retainership remuneration as per Prasar Bharati guidelines.

how_to_apply: Candidates who wish to apply for Prasar Bharati Part-Time Correspondent (PTC) Recruitment 2026 through offline mode should follow the steps below.
Read the official notification carefully before applying.
Download the application form from the official website: www.newsonair.gov.in.
Fill the application form carefully with correct information.
Attach self-attested copies of required documents such as photograph, signature, ID proof, and certificates.
Check all details carefully before submission.
Send the completed application form to the address mentioned in the notification through Speed Post / Registered Post or as instructed.
Ensure the application reaches before the last date: 07-07-2026.
Keep a photocopy of the submitted application form for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the Prasar Bharati Part-Time Correspondent (PTC) recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the application for the Prasar Bharati Part-Time Correspondent (PTC) recruitment is July 7, 2026.

faq.question: Q2. What is the eligibility criteria for the Part-Time Correspondent (PTC) post?
faq.answer: Candidates should have a PG Diploma/Degree in Journalism/Mass Media or be a graduate with at least two years of journalistic experience, along with computer knowledge and news-gathering devices.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The age limit for the Part-Time Correspondent (PTC) post is between 24 years and 50 years.

faq.question: Q4. Is there any application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No, there is no application fee for General/OBC and SC/ST categories for this recruitment.

faq.question: Q5. How can I apply for the Prasar Bharati Part-Time Correspondent (PTC) post?
faq.answer: Interested candidates need to apply offline by sending the duly filled application form along with required documents to the official address mentioned in the notification.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for this position?
faq.answer: The selection process for the Part-Time Correspondent (PTC) post will be based on an interview.

faq.question: Q7. What is the official website for Prasar Bharati recruitment?
faq.answer: The official website for Prasar Bharati recruitment is www.newsonair.gov.in.

faq.question: Q8. What type of job is this recruitment?
faq.answer: This is a contractual job opportunity for the Part-Time Correspondent (PTC) post.

faq.question: Q9. How many vacancies are available for the Part-Time Correspondent (PTC) post?
faq.answer: There are a total of 4 vacancies for the Part-Time Correspondent (PTC) post.

faq.question: Q10. Where is the job location for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The job location for this recruitment is Cuttack, Odisha.
